title: Pouteria hahniana
uses: Pouteria hahniana is a species of plant that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in its native Brazil. The extracts from this plant have been studied for their potential antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections. The medicinal uses of Pouteria hahniana are also attributed to its antioxidant and immunomodulatory properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and applications of this plant in modern medicine.
taxonomy: Pouteria hahniana is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the family Sapotaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sapote family. The Sapotaceae family includes around 900 species of trees and shrubs, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Pouteria hahniana is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, particularly in countries like Costa Rica and Panama. Within the Sapotaceae family, Pouteria hahniana is classified as a member of the genus Pouteria.
